'''Shatuun''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Kathol Outback/Legends|Kathol Outback]] region of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. The planet was covered in towering [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]] and plagued by fierce electrical storms. It was an isolated planet, seldom visited by travelers en route to [[Danoor]].

==History==
Shatuun was home to a civilization tens of thousands of years before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], which came under the influence of the [[Celestial/Legends|Celestials]]. This civilization later disappeared, apparently destroyed by the Celestials, leaving among their artifacts [[Ophidian grotesques]]. In [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Insmot Bowen]] compared the artifacts to similar artistic motifs found on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and [[Caulus Tertius]] as evidence of [[The galaxy/Legends|pan-galactic]] Celestial influence.<ref name="Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-the-celestials|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare'' Author's Cut — The Celestials|int=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut}}</ref>

The planet later became home to a mated pair of [[Duinuogwuin/Legends|Duinuogwuins]]. They had settled on planet in [[52 BBY/Legends|52 BBY]], intending to reproduce. After six [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] of settling in, they were finally getting around to the task as of [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]]. They were shy about receiving visitors, but were welcome of the company if their privacy was ensured.

==Appearances==
